summary: Cycle 98 hypothesis isolation CONFIRMED — SYSTEMIC, not card-pattern specific. Real Google Gemini analyzed SSN 880101-1234567 with detailed PII reasoning (extracted birthdate '1988년 1월 1일', century '20세기', gender '남성'). Same StreamGenerate endpoint, same real Google IPv6, same real LLM streaming as #659. Combined with #659 (credit card REGRESSION), both PII pattern types bypass engine through identical StreamGenerate path. Rules out card-pattern regex/normalization hypothesis. CONFIRMS endpoint-matcher root cause from #659.

network.duration_signature: long streaming = real LLM, NOT engine intercept
network.engine_intercept_evidence: ABSENT (no RST_STREAM, no small response, no engine sentinel)
network.background_polling_rows: 4 other StreamGenerate rows at 0.3 kB / 5-10 ms (background)

key_findings.1_hypothesis_isolation_systemic: Combined with #659, both PII pattern types (credit card AND SSN) bypass engine through identical StreamGenerate path. Pattern-specific (regex/normalization) hypothesis RULED OUT. Endpoint-matcher root cause CONFIRMED.
key_findings.2_production_log_match: Matches 2026-04-28 production log evidence: SSN 880101-1234567 reached engine on StreamGenerate but blocked=0. Same endpoint, same outcome.
key_findings.3_endpoint_matcher_fix_required: Engine endpoint matcher must be extended from `wrb_fr` to also capture `assistant.lamda.BardFrontendService/StreamGenerate`. Until then, ALL Gemini PII patterns leak.
key_findings.4_5th_strike_streak: 5th consecutive failure in cycle 95-98 gemini3 streak (#648/#650/#652/#659/#660). Pattern is consistent: ENDPOINT MATCHER mismatch.

operational_note.windows_mcp_gemini_caveat: windows-mcp Type/Click on Gemini's rich-textarea + send button silently failed (text appeared but submit click was no-op — likely Quill editor needs framework-level input event).
operational_note.workaround_that_worked: DevTools Console JS using `document.execCommand('insertText', ...)` + querySelector for `aria-label='메시지 보내기'` button + `.click()`. Required first typing `allow pasting` to defeat Chrome's anti-paste warning, and using `execCommand` instead of `innerHTML` to satisfy Trusted Types CSP.
operational_note.future_recommendation: Worth documenting in test-pc-worker SKILL.md for future Gemini scenarios (or any rich-textarea framework like Quill, Slate, ProseMirror).

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: SYSTEMIC endpoint matcher mismatch CONFIRMED. Engine targets `wrb_fr` but actual gemini3 traffic flows through `BardChatUi/data/assistant.lamda.BardFrontendService/StreamGenerate`. ALL PII patterns leak regardless of regex/normalization.
next_steps: URGENT: Update engine endpoint matcher to include `BardChatUi/data/assistant.lamda.BardFrontendService/StreamGenerate`
After matcher fix: re-verify SSN AND credit card patterns
Re-run Phase 1 HAR capture to confirm any other endpoint variations
User-side action: NO PII testing on gemini3 (any pattern) until matcher fix deployed (mission-critical)
Consider documenting Gemini rich-textarea workaround in test-pc-worker skill (execCommand insertText pattern)
recommendation.mission_critical: URGENT — gemini3 leaks ALL PII patterns to real Google LLM. Both card and SSN confirmed. Pattern-agnostic systemic failure.
recommendation.checklist_impact: Cycle 98 F2 Option β NOT verified (still untestable). Endpoint matcher fix is prerequisite for ALL further gemini3 hypothesis testing.
